title: Swish Gated Linear Unit
description: A gated feed-forward design that multiplies a value branch by a swish gate before projecting back.
openingSummary: The swish gated linear unit (SwiGLU) splits a feed-forward layer into value and gate branches, applies the sigmoid linear unit (SiLU) to the gate, and multiplies the two branches so the layer can filter features more selectively.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: The swish gated linear unit (SwiGLU) is a gated feed-forward design used in many modern language models. Instead of using one expanded hidden branch, the layer creates a value branch and a gate branch. The gate branch passes through SiLU, then scales the value branch element by element before the layer projects back to the model width.

whyItExists.title: Why It Exists
whyItExists.body: SwiGLU optimizes for a more selective dense hidden path. Instead of treating every hidden feature the same way, it lets a learned gate strengthen or suppress each feature before the final projection.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: The input state enters two learned projections. One branch carries candidate values. The other branch becomes a SiLU gate. The two branches multiply elementwise, and the layer then projects the result back to the model width. The path stays dense because every token still uses the same shared block.

mathOrComputeSchema.title: Math Or Compute Schema
mathOrComputeSchema.body: The dense baseline below shows the simpler one-branch feed-forward layer. The SwiGLU formula shows the extra gate that turns that same slot into a two-branch gated path.

comparedToNearbyModules.title: Compared To Nearby Modules
comparedToNearbyModules.body: SwiGLU stays closer to the standard dense feed-forward baseline than mixture of experts does. It changes how features are filtered inside one dense block rather than turning the slot into a sparse routed expert system.

exampleArchitectures.title: Example Architectures
exampleArchitectures.body: Many recent decoder-only language models use gated feed-forward designs like SwiGLU while keeping the same attention-then-feed-forward block structure.

limitationsAndTradeoffs.title: Limitations And Tradeoffs
limitationsAndTradeoffs.body: SwiGLU is still a dense block, so every token activates the whole path. It improves selectivity inside the layer, but it does not add sparse expert capacity the way mixture of experts does.

whyItStillMatters.title: Why It Still Matters
whyItStillMatters.body: SwiGLU appears often enough in modern model descriptions that readers benefit from seeing it as one of the default feed-forward baselines, not as a rare paper-only detail.

computeFlow.alt: Comparison graph showing a standard feed-forward layer versus a swish gated linear unit path with value and SiLU gate branches

swiglu.pathShape: Value branch multiplied by a SiLU gate branch
swiglu.activeCompute: All hidden units plus a learned gate
swiglu.mainTradeoff: More selective dense features, but still full dense compute

standard.pathShape: One dense expand -> activate -> project path
standard.activeCompute: All hidden units in one shared feed-forward block
standard.mainTradeoff: Simpler path, but less selective hidden filtering

moe.pathShape: Router selects a few expert MLPs, then merges them
moe.activeCompute: Only top-k experts activate for each token
moe.mainTradeoff: More capacity, but routing and sparse training become part of the design

ht.definition: Hidden state for token position t.

w1.definition: Projection into the wide hidden layer.

sigma.definition: Pointwise activation inside the dense hidden layer.

ht.definition: Hidden state for token position t.

wv.definition: Projection that builds the value branch.

wg.definition: Projection that builds the gate branch.

wo.definition: Projection back to model width after gating.

odot.definition: Elementwise multiplication between the value and gate branches.

swigluModule.label: SwiGLU
swigluModule.summary: This canonical node names the full SwiGLU module so readers can open the main docs page while the rest of the graph explains the internal branch structure.

gateActivation.label: SiLU
gateActivation.summary: This canonical SiLU node shows the activation that shapes the gate branch before the gate scales the value path.

gatedProduct.label: Elementwise multiply
gatedProduct.summary: This graph-local operator multiplies the value path by the SiLU-shaped gate, which is the moment the two branches become one SwiGLU update.
